- name: "hero-parallax.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/hero-parallax.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/hero-parallax"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "products"
      type: "Array"
      required: true
    - name: "product"
      type: "Object"
      required: true
    - name: "translate"
      type: "MotionValue<number>"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used as the main hero section of the homepage to showcase a collection of exquisite jewelry pieces. The parallax scroll effect will create an engaging and immersive experience for the user, drawing them into the world of luxury and craftsmanship."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/hero-parallax
    2. Description: A scroll effect with rotation, translation and opacity animations.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 5/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { HeroParallax } from "./ui/hero-parallax";
       
       export function HeroParallaxDemo() {
         return <HeroParallax products={products} />;
       }

- name: "hero-sections.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/hero-sections.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/hero-sections-free"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: false
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used as the main banner on the homepage to showcase a featured jewelry collection with a captivating image or video background."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/hero-section-demo-1
    2. Description: A set of hero sections ranging from simple to complex layouts, designed to be the first thing a user sees on a webpage.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 5/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { motion } from "framer-motion";
       import { HeroHighlight, Highlight } from "./hero-highlight";
       
       export function HeroSectionOne() {
         return (
           <HeroHighlight>
             <motion.h1
               initial={{
                 opacity: 0,
                 y: 20,
               }}
               animate={{
                 opacity: 1,
                 y: [20, -5, 0],
               }}
               transition={{
                 duration: 0.5,
                 ease: [0.4, 0.0, 0.2, 1],
               }}
               className="text-2xl px-4 md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-bold text-neutral-700 dark:text-white max-w-4xl leading-relaxed lg:leading-snug text-center mx-auto "
             >
               With our AI, you can launch your website in hours, not days.{" "}
               <Highlight className="text-black dark:text-white">
                 Try our best in class, state of the art, cutting edge AI tools to get
                 your website up.
               </Highlight>
             </motion.h1>
           </HeroHighlight>
         );
       }

- name: "images-slider.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/images-slider.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/images-slider"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "images"
      type: "string[]"
      required: true
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "overlay"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "overlayClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "autoplay"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
    - name: "direction"
      type: ""up" | "down""
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component is perfect for a hero section on a luxury jewelry website, showcasing stunning high-resolution images of necklaces, rings, and other pieces. The autoplay feature can create a dynamic and immersive experience for visitors, drawing them into the world of Vault Maison."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/images-slider
    2. Description: A full-page slider with images that can be navigated with the keyboard. It provides a visually engaging way to showcase a collection of images.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 5/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { ImagesSlider } from "./ui/images-slider";
       
       const MyComponent = () => {
         const images = [
           "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1485433592409-9018e83a1f0d?q=80&w=1887&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1483982258113-b72862e6cff6?q=80&w=1974&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1482189349482-3da1252e70a4?q=80&w=1974&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
         ];
         return (
           <ImagesSlider className="h-[40rem]" images={images}>
             <div className="z-50 flex flex-col justify-center items-center">
               <p className="font-bold text-xl md:text-6xl text-center bg-clip-text text-transparent bg-gradient-to-b from-neutral-50 to-neutral-400 py-4">
                 The hero section slideshow <br /> for your creative agency
               </p>
               <button className="px-4 py-2 backdrop-blur-sm border bg-emerald-300/10 border-emerald-500/20 text-white mx-auto text-center rounded-full relative mt-4">
                 <span>View More &rarr;</span>
                 <div className="absolute inset-x-0  h-px -bottom-px bg-gradient-to-r from-transparent via-emerald-500 to-transparent" />
               </button>
             </div>
           </ImagesSlider>
         );
       };

  path: "src/components/ui/parallax-hero-images.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"

  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "tailwindcss"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "images"
      type: "string[]"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "imageClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "variant"
      type: ""default" | "edge-focus""
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This could be used as the main hero section on the homepage, showcasing a collection of exquisite jewelry pieces with a captivating parallax effect that draws the user in."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/parallax-hero-images
    2. Description: A mouse-driven parallax effect, perfect for hero sections. Images blur fade in and move at different depths, creating an engaging and visually appealing experience.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 5/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { HeroParallax } from "@/components/ui/hero-parallax";
       
       export default function Home() {
         const products = [
           { title: "Product 1", link: "#", thumbnail: "/image1.png" },
           { title: "Product 2", link: "#", thumbnail: "/image2.png" },
           { title: "Product 3", link: "#", thumbnail: "/image3.png" },
         ];
         return <HeroParallax products={products} />;
       }

- name: "3d-card-effect.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/3d-card-effect.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/3d-card-effect"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "CardContainer"
      type: "children: React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "containerClassName"
      type: "string; CardBody: children: React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string; CardItem: as: React.ElementType"
      required: true
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "translateX"
      type: "number | string"
      required: true
    - name: "translateY"
      type: "number | string"
      required: true
    - name: "translateZ"
      type: "number | string"
      required: true
    - name: "rotateX"
      type: "number | string"
      required: true
    - name: "rotateY"
      type: "number | string"
      required: true
    - name: "rotateZ"
      type: "number | string"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component is highly suitable for a luxury jewelry website. It can be used to create an engaging and interactive product showcase for featured jewelry pieces, allowing users to see different angles and details in a dynamic way. It would also be effective for highlighting special collections or new arrivals."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/3d-card
    2. Description: A card perspective effect where hovering over the card elevates card elements, creating a 3D visual effect. The component allows for translating and rotating individual items within the card on hover.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       
       "use client";
       
       import Image from "next/image";
       import React from "react";
       import { CardBody, CardContainer, CardItem } from "@/components/ui/3d-card";
       import Link from "next/link";
       
       export function ThreeDCardDemo() {
         return (
           <CardContainer className="inter-var">
             <CardBody className="bg-gray-50 relative group/card  dark:hover:shadow-2xl dark:hover:shadow-emerald-500/[0.1] dark:bg-black dark:border-white/[0.2] border-black/[0.1] w-auto sm:w-[30rem] h-auto rounded-xl p-6 border  ">
               <CardItem
                 translateZ="50"
                 className="text-xl font-bold text-neutral-600 dark:text-white"
               >
                 Make things float in air
               </CardItem>
               <CardItem
                 as="p"
                 translateZ="60"
                 className="text-neutral-500 text-sm max-w-sm mt-2 dark:text-neutral-300"
               >
                 Hover over this card to unleash the power of CSS perspective
               </CardItem>
               <CardItem translateZ="100" className="w-full mt-4">
                 <Image
                   src="/images/example.png"
                   height="1000"
                   width="1000"
                   className="h-60 w-full object-cover rounded-xl group-hover/card:shadow-xl"
                   alt="thumbnail"
                 />
               </CardItem>
               <div className="flex justify-between items-center mt-20">
                 <CardItem
                   translateZ={20}
                   as={Link}
                   href="https://twitter.com/mannupaaji"
                   target="__blank"
                   className="px-4 py-2 rounded-xl text-xs font-normal dark:text-white"
                 >
                   Try now →
                 </CardItem>
                 <CardItem
                   translateZ={20}
                   as="button"
                   className="px-4 py-2 rounded-xl bg-black dark:bg-white dark:text-black text-white text-xs font-bold"
                 >
                   Sign up
                 </CardItem>
               </div>
             </CardBody>
           </CardContainer>
         );
       }

- name: "animated-testimonials.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/animated-testimonials.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/animated-testimonials"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "testimonials"
      type: "Array<{ quote: string; name: string; designation: string; src: string }>"
      required: true
    - name: "autoplay"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "Showcase testimonials from high-profile clients or brand ambassadors to enhance the brand's prestige and desirability."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/animated-testimonials
    2. Description: A minimalist testimonials section that animates through a series of quotes, names, and images. The component provides a clean and engaging way to display customer feedback or endorsements.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { AnimatedTestimonials } from "@/components/ui/animated-testimonials";
       
       export function AnimatedTestimonialsDemo() {
         const testimonials = [
           {
             quote:
               "The attention to detail and innovative features have completely transformed our workflow. This is exactly what we've been looking for.",
             name: "Sarah Chen",
             designation: "Product Manager at TechFlow",
             src: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1535713875002-d1d0cf377fde?q=80&w=3560&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           },
           {
             quote:
               "Implementation was seamless and the results exceeded our expectations. The platform is a game-changer for our team.",
             name: "Michael Rodriguez",
             designation: "CTO at InnovateSphere",
             src: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?q=80&w=3540&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           },
           {
             quote:
               "This solution has significantly improved our team's productivity. The intuitive interface and powerful features are a winning combination.",
             name: "Emily Watson",
             designation: "Operations Director at CloudScale",
           },
         ];
         return (
           <div className="flex h-[40rem] w-full flex-col items-center justify-center overflow-hidden rounded-md bg-black/[0.96] antialiased">
             <AnimatedTestimonials testimonials={testimonials} />
           </div>
         );
       }

    4. Basic Usage:
       import { AuroraBackground } from "@/components/ui/aurora-background";
       
       <AuroraBackground>
         <motion.div
           initial={{ opacity: 0.0, y: 40 }}
           whileInView={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
           transition={{ delay: 0.3, duration: 0.8, ease: "easeInOut" }}
           className="relative flex flex-col gap-4 items-center justify-center px-4"
         >
           <div className="text-3xl md:text-7xl font-bold dark:text-white text-center">
             Background lights are cool you know.
           </div>
           <div className="font-extralight text-base md:text-4xl dark:text-neutral-200 py-4">
             And this, is chemical burn.
           </div>
           <button className="bg-black dark:bg-white rounded-full w-fit text-white dark:text-black px-4 py-2">
             Debug now
           </button>
         </motion.div>
       </AuroraBackground>

           <div className="h-[40rem] w-full rounded-md bg-neutral-950 relative flex flex-col items-center justify-center antialiased">

       export function BackgroundLinesDemo() {
         return (
           <BackgroundLines className="flex items-center justify-center w-full flex-col px-4">
             <h2 className="bg-clip-text text-transparent text-center bg-gradient-to-b from-neutral-50 to-neutral-400">
               Sanjana Airlines, <br /> Sajana Textiles.
             </h2>
             <p className="max-w-xl mx-auto text-sm md:text-lg text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-400">
               Get the best advices from our experts, including expert artists,
               painters, marathon enthusiasts and RDX, totally free.
             </p>
           </BackgroundLines>
         );
       }

- name: "bento-grid.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/bento-grid.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/bento-grid"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "items"
      type: "{title: string"
      required: true
    - name: "description"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "header"
      type: "ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "icon"
      type: "ReactNode}"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used to create a visually engaging 'Our Story' or 'Craftsmanship' section on a luxury jewelry website, where each grid item can showcase a different aspect of the brand's heritage, values, or the intricate process of creating their jewelry. It could also be used to feature different collections or highlight key pieces in a non-traditional, editorial-style layout."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/bento-grid
    2. Description: A skewed grid layout with Title, description and a header component. It's a modern and visually appealing way to display a collection of items, each with its own content and optional header.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { BentoGrid, BentoGridItem } from "@/components/ui/bento-grid";
       
       export function BentoGridDemo() {
         return (
           <BentoGrid className="max-w-4xl mx-auto">
             {items.map((item, i) => (
               <BentoGridItem
                 key={i}
                 title={item.title}
                 description={item.description}
                 header={item.header}
                 icon={item.icon}
               />
             ))}
           </BentoGrid>
         );
       }

- name: "canvas-reveal-effect.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/canvas-reveal-effect.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/canvas-reveal-effect"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "animationSpeed"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "opacities"
      type: "number[]"
      required: true
    - name: "colors"
      type: "number[][]"
      required: true
    - name: "containerClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "dotSize"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "showGradient"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This effect could be used to reveal high-resolution images of jewelry pieces on a product grid. As the user hovers over a product, the canvas reveal effect would create an elegant and engaging transition, drawing attention to the intricate details of the jewelry."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx aceternity-ui@latest add canvas-reveal-effect
    2. Description: A dot background that expands on hover, creating a reveal effect. The component uses HTML5 canvas to render a grid of dots that animate and change opacity and size based on mouse interaction, revealing the content underneath.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { CanvasRevealEffect } from "@/components/ui/canvas-reveal-effect";
       
       <CanvasRevealEffect
         animationSpeed={5.1}
         containerClassName="bg-emerald-900"
         colors={[[125, 211, 252]]}
         dotSize={2}
       />

  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/card-hover-effect"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "tailwindcss", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]

  props:
    - name: "items"

    - name: "link"

      type: "string"
      required: false

    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/card-hover-effect
    2. Description: The Card Hover Effect component is a visually engaging way to display a grid of cards. When a user hovers over a card, a colored background smoothly slides to highlight the selected card, providing a clean and modern interactive experience.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5

       import { HoverEffect } from "./ui/card-hover-effect";

         return (

             <HoverEffect items={projects} />
           </div>
         );
       }
       export const projects = [
         {
           title: "Stripe",
           description:
             "A technology company that builds economic infrastructure for the internet.",
           link: "https://stripe.com",
         },
         {
           title: "Netflix",
           description:
             "A streaming service that offers a wide variety of award-winning TV shows, movies, anime, documentaries, and more on thousands of internet-connected devices.",
           link: "https://netflix.com",
         },
       ];

                 <Step title="Verify your identity" />

                 information and data.
               </p>

         );
       }

  ai_instructions: |

    4. Basic Usage:
       import type { Config } from "tailwindcss";
        
       const config: Config = {
         content: [
           "./pages/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}",
           "./components/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}",
           "./app/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}",
         ],
         theme: {
           extend: {
             animation: {
               move: "move 5s linear infinite",
             },
             keyframes: {
               move: {
                 "0%": { transform: "translateX(-200px)" },
                 "100%": { transform: "translateX(200px)" },
               },
             },
           },
         },
         plugins: [],
       };
        
       export default config;

- name: "direction-aware-hover.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/direction-aware-hover.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/direction-aware-hover"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "tailwindcss"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "imageUrl"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode or string"
      required: true
    - name: "childrenClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: false
    - name: "imageClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: false
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used to create an elegant and interactive product showcase. When a user hovers over a piece of jewelry, details like the name, price, or a short description can be revealed in a smooth, direction-aware manner, enhancing the luxury feel of the website."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/direction-aware-hover
    2. Description: A direction-aware hover effect using Framer Motion, Tailwind CSS, and JavaScript. When a user hovers over an element, an overlay with additional information appears from the direction the user's mouse enters.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { DirectionAwareHover } from "../ui/direction-aware-hover";
       
       export function DirectionAwareHoverDemo() {
         const imageUrl = "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1663765970236-f2acfde22237?q=80&w=3542&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D";
         return (
           <div className="h-[40rem] relative flex items-center justify-center">
             <DirectionAwareHover imageUrl={imageUrl}>
               <p className="font-bold text-xl">In the mountains</p>
               <p className="font-normal text-sm">$1299 / night</p>
             </DirectionAwareHover>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "expandable-cards.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/expandable-cards.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/expandable-card"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "cards"
      type: "{id: number"
      required: true
    - name: "title"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "description"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "thumbnail"
      type: "string}[]"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: false
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used to showcase featured jewelry pieces or collections on the homepage. A click can expand the card to show a larger image, details about the piece, and a call-to-action button."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/expandable-card-demo-standard @aceternity/expandable-card-demo-grid
    2. Description: A component that displays a list of cards, which expand upon being clicked to reveal additional information or content. The expanded card can be closed by clicking outside of it or pressing the Escape key.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       '''"use client";
       import React, { useEffect, useId, useRef, useState } from "react";
       import { AnimatePresence, motion } from "framer-motion";
       import { useOutsideClick } from "@/hooks/use-outside-click";
       
       export function ExpandableCardDemo() {
         const [active, setActive] = useState<{
           id: number;
           title: string;
           description: string;
           thumbnail: string;
         } | null>(null);
         const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
         const id = useId();
       
         useEffect(() => {
           function onKeyDown(event: KeyboardEvent) {
             if (event.key === "Escape") {
               setActive(null);
             }
           }
       
           if (active && typeof active === "object") {
             document.body.style.overflow = "hidden";
           } else {
             document.body.style.overflow = "auto";
           }
       
           window.addEventListener("keydown", onKeyDown);
           return () => window.removeEventListener("keydown", onKeyDown);
         }, [active]);
       
         useOutsideClick(ref, () => setActive(null));
       
         return (
           <>
             <AnimatePresence>
               {active && typeof active === "object" && (
                 <motion.div
                   initial={{ opacity: 0 }}
                   animate={{ opacity: 1 }}
                   exit={{ opacity: 0 }}
                   className="fixed inset-0 bg-black/20 h-full w-full z-10"
                 />
               )}
             </AnimatePresence>
             <div className="relative w-full max-w-lg mx-auto h-[40rem] [perspective:1000px]">
               <AnimatePresence>
                 {active && typeof active === "object" ? (
                   <motion.div
                     initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 100, scale: 0.9 }}
                     animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0, scale: 1 }}
                     exit={{ opacity: 0, y: 100, scale: 0.9 }}
                     transition={{ type: "spring", stiffness: 100, damping: 20 }}
                     className="absolute inset-0 z-20"
                   >
                     <div ref={ref} className="h-full w-full bg-white dark:bg-zinc-900 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden shadow-lg">
                       <div className="relative h-1/2 w-full">
                         <img
                           src={active.thumbnail}
                           alt={active.title}
                           className="h-full w-full object-cover"
                         />
                         <button
                           onClick={() => setActive(null)}
                           className="absolute top-4 right-4 text-white bg-black/50 rounded-full h-8 w-8 flex items-center justify-center"
                         >
                           &times;
                         </button>
                       </div>
                       <div className="p-4">
                         <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-zinc-800 dark:text-zinc-100">
                           {active.title}
                         </h2>
                         <p className="text-sm text-zinc-600 dark:text-zinc-400 mt-2">
                           {active.description}
                         </p>
                       </div>
                     </div>
                   </motion.div>
                 ) : null}
               </AnimatePresence>
               <ul className="flex flex-col space-y-4">
                 {cards.map((card, index) => (
                   <motion.li
                     key={card.id}
                     layoutId={`card-${card.id}-${id}`}
                     onClick={() => setActive(card)}
                     className="p-4 bg-white dark:bg-zinc-900 rounded-xl shadow-md cursor-pointer"
                   >
                     <div className="flex items-center space-x-4">
                       <img
                         src={card.thumbnail}
                         alt={card.title}
                         className="h-16 w-16 rounded-lg object-cover"
                       />
                       <div>
                         <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-zinc-800 dark:text-zinc-100">
                           {card.title}
                         </h3>
                         <p className="text-sm text-zinc-600 dark:text-zinc-400">
                           {card.description}
                         </p>
                       </div>
                     </div>
                   </motion.li>
                 ))}
               </ul>
             </div>
           </>
         );
       }
       
       const cards = [
         {
           id: 1,
           title: "Summertime Sadness",
           description: "Lana Del Rey",
           thumbnail: "https://i.scdn.co/image/ab67616d0000b2738b52c6b9bc4e43d873861a6e",
         },
         {
           id: 2,
           title: "Mitran Di Chhatri",
           description: "Babbu Maan",
           thumbnail: "https://i.scdn.co/image/ab67616d0000b27323255539305e2f325d9b719e",
         },
         {
           id: 3,
           title: "For Whom The Bell Tolls",
           description: "Metallica",
           thumbnail: "https://i.scdn.co/image/ab67616d0000b273e8b066f70c20655b3259247c",
         },
         {
           id: 4,
           title: "Stairway To Heaven",
           description: "Led Zeppelin",
           thumbnail: "https://i.scdn.co/image/ab67616d0000b27355c536353392358133845079",
         },
         {
           id: 5,
           title: "Toh Phir Aao",
           description: "Mustafa Zahid",
           thumbnail: "https://i.scdn.co/image/ab67616d0000b2738b52c6b9bc4e43d873861a6e",
         },
       ];'''

- name: "feature-sections.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/feature-sections.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/feature-sections-free"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge", "@tabler/icons-react"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "The"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "the"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "and"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "`title`"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "`description`"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "`header`"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "`icon`"
      type: "any"
      required: true
    - name: "and"
      type: "any"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "Ideal for creating an elegant 'Our Craftsmanship' or 'The Vault Maison Difference' section, using the Bento Grid to visually narrate the story behind jewelry pieces, detailing materials, sourcing, and unique design features."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/features-section-demo-1 @aceternity/features-section-demo-2 @aceternity/features-section-demo-3
    2. Description: A collection of versatile feature sections, including Bento Grids and simpler card-based layouts. These components are designed to stylishly showcase product features, company strengths, or other key information in a structured and visually engaging format.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       This page provides three different feature section components. The primary one demonstrated is a Bento Grid layout. The code for the Bento Grid component itself is not directly provided, but its usage is shown. Here is an example of how to use the `BentoGrid` and `BentoGridItem` components that would be added by the CLI command:
       
       ```jsx
       import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
       import React from "react";
       import { BentoGrid, BentoGridItem } from "@/components/ui/bento-grid";
       import {
         IconArrowWaveRightUp,
         IconBoxAlignRightFilled,
         IconBoxAlignTopLeft,
         IconClipboardCopy,
         IconFileBroken,
         IconSignature,
         IconTableColumn,
       } from "@tabler/icons-react";
       
       export function BentoGridDemo() {
         return (
           <BentoGrid className="max-w-4xl mx-auto">
             {items.map((item, i) => (
               <BentoGridItem
                 key={i}
                 title={item.title}
                 description={item.description}
                 header={item.header}
                 icon={item.icon}
                 className={i === 3 || i === 6 ? "md:col-span-2" : ""}
               />
             ))}
           </BentoGrid>
         );
       }
       
       // The `items` array with data for each grid item would be defined here.
       ```

           {

             src: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600271772470-bd22a42787b3?q=80&w=3072&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           },

             src: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505142468610-359e7d316e0?q=80&w=3070&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           },
           {
             title: "Camping is for pros",
             src: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1486915309851-b0cc1f8a0084?q=80&w=3387&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
           },
           {

- name: "glowing-effect.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/glowing-effect.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/glowing-effect"
  npm_dependencies: ["lucide-react"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "blur"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "inactiveZone"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "proximity"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "spread"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "variant"
      type: ""default" | "white""
      required: true
    - name: "glow"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "disabled"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
    - name: "movementDuration"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "borderWidth"
      type: "number"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "The glowing effect can be used to create a premium and eye-catching highlight for featured jewelry pieces or special collection cards on the Vault Maison website. It can draw user attention to new arrivals or exclusive items, enhancing the luxurious feel of the user interface."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/glowing-effect
    2. Description: A border glowing effect that adapts to any container or card. The glow is interactive and follows the user's cursor, creating a visually appealing highlight on hover.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       
       "use client";
       import React from "react";
       import { GlowingEffect } from "@/components/ui/glowing-effect";
       import { Box, Lock, Search, Settings, Sparkles } from "lucide-react";
       
       export function GlowingEffectDemo() {
         return (
           <div className="flex items-center justify-center bg-black">
             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-2 md:grid-rows-3 lg:grid-cols-3">
               <GlowingEffect>
                 <div className="p-4">
                   <Lock className="h-4 w-4 text-neutral-500" />
                   <p className="mt-2 text-lg font-semibold">Do things the right way</p>
                   <p className="mt-2 text-sm text-neutral-500">
                     Running out of copy so I'll write anything.
                   </p>
                 </div>
               </GlowingEffect>
             </div>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "grid-and-dot-backgrounds.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/grid-and-dot-backgrounds.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/grid-and-dot-backgrounds"
  npm_dependencies: ["motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "Not"
      type: "any"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "Can be used as a subtle background for the entire website or for specific sections to add a clean, modern, and luxurious feel without distracting from the jewelry products."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx aceternity-ui@latest add grid-and-dot-backgrounds
    2. Description: A simple grid and dots background to make your sections stand out.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
       import React from "react";
        
       export function GridBackgroundDemo() {
         return (
           <div className="relative flex h-[50rem] w-full items-center justify-center bg-white dark:bg-black">
             <div
               className={cn(
                 "absolute inset-0",
                 "[background-size:40px_40px]",
                 "[background-image:linear-gradient(to_right,#e4e4e7_1px,transparent_1px),linear-gradient(to_bottom,#e4e4e7_1px,transparent_1px)]",
                 "dark:[background-image:linear-gradient(to_right,#262626_1px,transparent_1px),linear-gradient(to_bottom,#262626_1px,transparent_1px)]",
               )}
             />
             {/* Radial gradient for the container to give a faded look */}
             <div className="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center bg-white [mask-image:radial-gradient(ellipse_at_center,transparent_20%,black)] dark:bg-black"></div>
             <p className="relative z-20 bg-gradient-to-b from-neutral-200 to-neutral-500 bg-clip-text py-8 text-4xl font-bold text-transparent sm:text-7xl">
               Backgrounds
             </p>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "hero-highlight.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/hero-highlight.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/hero-highlight"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "@aceternity/hero-highlight"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: false
    - name: "containerClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: false
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used to create a visually appealing hero section for the Vault Maison website, highlighting a key message or a new jewelry collection. The subtle text highlight effect adds a touch of elegance and sophistication."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/hero-highlight
    2. Description: A background effect with a text highlight component, perfect for hero sections.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { motion } from "framer-motion";
       import { HeroHighlight, Highlight } from "../ui/hero-highlight";
       
       export function HeroHighlightDemo() {
         return (
           <HeroHighlight>
             <motion.h1
               initial={{
                 opacity: 0,
                 y: 20,
               }}
               animate={{
                 opacity: 1,
                 y: [20, -5, 0],
               }}
               transition={{
                 duration: 0.5,
                 ease: [0.4, 0.0, 0.2, 1],
               }}
               className="text-2xl px-4 md:text-4xl lg:text-5xl font-bold text-neutral-700 dark:text-white max-w-4xl leading-relaxed lg:leading-snug text-center mx-auto "
             >
               With insomnia, nothing's real. Everything is far away. Everything is a copy, of a copy, of a copy.
             </motion.h1>
           </HeroHighlight>
         );
       }

       '''"use client";
       import React from "react";
       import { motion } from "framer-motion";
       import { LampContainer } from "./ui/lamp";

         return (
           <LampContainer>
             <motion.h1
               initial={{ opacity: 0.5, y: 100 }}
               whileInView={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
               transition={{
                 delay: 0.3,
                 duration: 0.8,
                 ease: "easeInOut",
               }}
               className="mt-8 bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-300 to-slate-500 py-4 bg-clip-text text-center text-4xl font-medium tracking-tight text-transparent md:text-7xl"
             >
               Build lamps <br /> the right way
             </motion.h1>
           </LampContainer>
         );
       }
       '''

  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/lens"

      required: false

      type: "{ x: number"
      required: false
    - name: "y"
      type: "number }"
      required: true
    - name: "isStatic"
      type: "boolean"
      required: false
    - name: "isFocusing"
      type: "() => void"
      required: false
    - name: "hovering"
      type: "boolean"
      required: false
    - name: "setHovering"
      type: "(hovering: boolean) => void"
      required: false

    - "This component can be used to provide a detailed, zoomed-in view of jewelry, allowing customers to inspect the craftsmanship and details of each piece, which is crucial for luxury items."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/lens
    2. Description: A lens component to zoom into images, videos, or practically anything. It provides a magnified view of content, which can be useful for showcasing product details.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { useState } from "react";
       import { Lens } from "../ui/lens";
       import { motion } from "framer-motion";
       import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
       
       export function LensDemo() {
         const [hovering, setHovering] = useState(false);
       
         return (
           <div className="w-full relative rounded-3xl overflow-hidden max-w-md mx-auto bg-gradient-to-b from-purple-500 to-indigo-500">
             <Rays />
             <div className="relative z-10">
               <Lens hovering={hovering} setHovering={setHovering}>
                 <img
                   src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1713869820987-519844949a9a?q=80&w=350&auto=format&fit=crop"
                   alt="image"
                   width={500}
                   height={500}
                   className="rounded-2xl"
                 />
               </Lens>
             </div>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "meteor-effect.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/meteor-effect.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/meteors"
  npm_dependencies: ["tailwindcss", "framer-motion"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "number"
      type: "number"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used to create a captivating and magical background for featured product sections or promotional banners on a luxury jewelry website, adding a touch of elegance and wonder."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/meteors
    2. Description: A customizable component that adds animated meteor effects to the background of a container, creating a visually engaging and dynamic look.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { Meteors } from "@/components/ui/meteors";
       
       export function MeteorsDemo() {
         return (
           <div className="">
             <div className=" w-full relative max-w-xs">
               <div className="absolute inset-0 h-full w-full bg-gradient-to-r from-blue-500 to-teal-500 transform scale-[0.80] bg-red-500 rounded-full blur-3xl" />
               <div className="relative shadow-xl bg-gray-900 border border-gray-800  px-4 py-8 h-full overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex flex-col justify-end items-start">
                 <div className="h-5 w-5 rounded-full border flex items-center justify-center mb-4 border-gray-500">
                   <svg
                     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
                     fill="none"
                     viewBox="0 0 24 24"
                     stroke-width="1.5"
                     stroke="currentColor"
                     className="h-2 w-2 text-gray-300"
                   >
                     <path
                       stroke-linecap="round"
                       stroke-linejoin="round"
                       d="M4.5 4.5l15 15m0 0V8.25M19.5 19.5H8.25"
                     />
                   </svg>
                 </div>
       
                 <h1 className="font-bold text-xl text-white mb-4 relative z-50">
                   Meteors because they're cool
                 </h1>
       
                 <p className="font-normal text-base text-slate-500 mb-4 relative z-50">
                   I don't know what to write so I'll just paste something cool here. One more sentence because lorem ipsum is just unacceptable. Won't ChatGPT the shit out of this.
                 </p>
       
                 <button className="border px-4 py-1 rounded-lg  border-gray-500 text-gray-300">
                   Explore
                 </button>
       
                 {/* Meaty part - Meteor effect */}
                 <Meteors number={20} />
               </div>
             </div>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "placeholders-and-vanish-input.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/placeholders-and-vanish-input.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/placeholders-and-vanish-input"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "placeholders"
      type: "string[]"
      required: true
    - name: "onChange"
      type: "(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => void"
      required: true
    - name: "onSubmit"
      type: "(e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => void"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This could be used as a creative and engaging search bar on a luxury jewelry website, perhaps for searching for specific collections, designers, or types of jewelry. The animated placeholders could showcase different search queries or highlight popular items."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/placeholders-and-vanish-input
    2. Description: A dynamic input field that cycles through a series of placeholder strings and vanishes upon submission. This component is visually engaging, drawing inspiration from OpenAI's hero section and Rauno's 'Craft of Vanish Input' for a modern, interactive user experience.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       '''
       "use client";
       
       import { PlaceholdersAndVanishInput } from "@/components/ui/placeholders-and-vanish-input";
       
       export function PlaceholdersAndVanishInputDemo() {
         const placeholders = [
           "What's the first rule of Fight Club?",
           "Who is Tyler Durden?",
           "Where is Andrew Laeddis Hiding?",
           "Write a Javascript method to reverse a string",
           "How to assemble your own PC?",
         ];
       
         const handleChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
           console.log(e.target.value);
         };
         const onSubmit = (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => {
           e.preventDefault();
           console.log("submitted");
         };
         return (
           <div className="h-[40rem] flex flex-col justify-center  items-center px-4">
             <h2 className="mb-10 sm:mb-20 text-xl text-center sm:text-5xl dark:text-white text-black">
               Ask Aceternity UI Anything
             </h2>
             <PlaceholdersAndVanishInput
               placeholders={placeholders}
               onChange={handleChange}
               onSubmit={onSubmit}
             />
           </div>
         );
       }
       '''

       }

- name: "sidebar.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/sidebar"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "react", "tailwindcss"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "SidebarProvider"
      type: "children: React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "open"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
    - name: "setOpen"
      type: "React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<boolean>>; Sidebar: children: React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "open"
      type: "boolean"
      required: true
    - name: "setOpen"
      type: "React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<boolean>>"
      required: true
    - name: "animate"
      type: "boolean; SidebarBody: props: React.ComponentProps<typeof motion.div>; DesktopSidebar: className: string"
      required: true
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "props"
      type: "React.ComponentProps<typeof motion.div>; MobileSidebar: className: string"
      required: true
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
    - name: "props"
      type: "React.ComponentProps<"div">; SidebarLink: link: Links"
      required: true
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "props"
      type: "LinkProps; Links (interface): label: string"
      required: true
    - name: "href"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "icon"
      type: "React.JSX.Element | React.ReactNode; SidebarContextProps (interface): open: boolean"
      required: true
    - name: "setOpen"
      type: "React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<boolean>>"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "Can be used for customer account navigation (e.g., order history, saved items, profile settings) or as a filter panel on a product listing page."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/sidebar
    2. Description: An expandable sidebar that appears on hover, is mobile-responsive, and supports dark mode. It's a common UI pattern for navigation in web applications.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { Sidebar, SidebarBody, SidebarLink } from "@aceternity/sidebar";
       import {
         IconArrowLeft,
         IconBrandTabler,
         IconSettings,
         IconUserBolt,
       } from "@tabler/icons-react";
       import { motion } from "framer-motion";
       import React, { useState } from "react";
       import { Link } from "react-router-dom";
       
       const links = [
         {
           label: "Dashboard",
           href: "#",
           icon: (
             <IconBrandTabler className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200 h-5 w-5 flex-shrink-0" />
           ),
         },
         {
           label: "Profile",
           href: "#",
           icon: (
             <IconUserBolt className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200 h-5 w-5 flex-shrink-0" />
           ),
         },
         {
           label: "Settings",
           href: "#",
           icon: (
             <IconSettings className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200 h-5 w-5 flex-shrink-0" />
           ),
         },
       ];
       
       export function SidebarDemo() {
         const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
         return (
           <SidebarProvider>
             <Sidebar open={open} setOpen={setOpen} animate={true}>
               <SidebarBody className="justify-between gap-10">
                 <div className="flex flex-col flex-1">
                   {open ? <Logo /> : <LogoIcon />}
                   <div className="mt-8 flex flex-col gap-2">
                     {links.map((link, idx) => (
                       <SidebarLink key={idx} link={link} />
                     ))}
                   </div>
                 </div>
                 <div>
                   <SidebarLink
                     link={{
                       label: "Logout",
                       href: "#",
                       icon: (
                         <IconArrowLeft className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-200 h-5 w-5 flex-shrink-0" />
                       ),
                     }}
                   />
                 </div>
               </SidebarBody>
             </Sidebar>
           </SidebarProvider>
         );
       }

- name: "signup-form.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/signup-form.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/signup-form"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge", "@tabler/icons-react"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "type"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "...props"
      type: "React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>"
      required: true
    - name: "ref"
      type: "React.Ref<HTMLInputElement>"
      required: true
    - name: "...props"
      type: "React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof LabelPrimitive.Root>"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used for creating exclusive member accounts, signing up for a newsletter to receive updates on new collections, or for pre-ordering exclusive jewelry pieces."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/signup-form-demo
    2. Description: A customizable form built on top of shadcn's input and label, with a touch of framer motion.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       
       "use client";
       import React from "react";
       import { Label } from "../ui/label";
       import { Input } from "../ui/input";
       import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
       import {
         IconBrandGithub,
         IconBrandGoogle,
         IconBrandOnlyfans,
       } from "@tabler/icons-react";
       
       export function SignupFormDemo() {
         const handleSubmit = (e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => {
           e.preventDefault();
           console.log("Form submitted");
         };
         return (
           <div className="max-w-md w-full mx-auto rounded-none md:rounded-2xl p-4 md:p-8 shadow-input bg-white dark:bg-black">
             <h2 className="font-bold text-xl text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-200">
               Welcome to Aceternity
             </h2>
             <p className="text-neutral-600 text-sm max-w-sm mt-2 dark:text-neutral-300">
               Login to aceternity if you can because we don&apos;t have a login flow
               yet
             </p>
       
             <form className="my-8" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
               <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row space-y-2 md:space-y-0 md:space-x-2 mb-4">
                 <LabelInputContainer>
                   <Label htmlFor="firstname">First name</Label>
                   <Input id="firstname" placeholder="Tyler" type="text" />
                 </LabelInputContainer>
                 <LabelInputContainer>
                   <Label htmlFor="lastname">Last name</Label>
                   <Input id="lastname" placeholder="Durden" type="text" />
                 </LabelInputContainer>
               </div>
               <LabelInputContainer className="mb-4">
                 <Label htmlFor="email">Email Address</Label>
                 <Input id="email" placeholder="projectmayhem@fc.com" type="email" />
               </LabelInputContainer>
               <LabelInputContainer className="mb-4">
                 <Label htmlFor="password">Password</Label>
                 <Input id="password" placeholder="••••••••" type="password" />
               </LabelInputContainer>
               <LabelInputContainer className="mb-8">
                 <Label htmlFor="twitterpassword">Your twitter password</Label>
                 <Input
                   id="twitterpassword"
                   placeholder="••••••••"
                   type="twitterpassword"
                 />
               </LabelInputContainer>
       
               <button
                 className="bg-gradient-to-br relative group/btn from-black dark:from-zinc-900 dark:to-zinc-900 to-neutral-600 block dark:bg-zinc-800 w-full text-white rounded-md h-10 font-medium shadow-[0px_1px_0px_0px_#ffffff40_inset,0px_-1px_0px_0px_#ffffff40_inset]_dark:shadow-[0px_1px_0px_0px_var(--zinc-800)_inset,0px_-1px_0px_0px_var(--zinc-800)_inset]"
                 type="submit"
               >
                 Sign up &rarr;
                 <BottomGradient />
               </button>
       
               <div className="bg-gradient-to-r from-transparent via-neutral-300 dark:via-neutral-700 to-transparent my-8 h-[1px] w-full" />
       
               <div className="flex flex-col space-y-4">
                 <button
                   className=" relative group/btn flex space-x-2 items-center justify-start px-4 w-full text-black rounded-md h-10 font-medium shadow-input bg-gray-50 dark:bg-zinc-900 dark:shadow-[0px_0px_1px_1px_var(--neutral-800)]"
                   type="submit"
                 >
                   <IconBrandGithub className="h-4 w-4 text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300" />
                   <span className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 text-sm">
                     GitHub
                   </span>
                   <BottomGradient />
                 </button>
                 <button
                   className=" relative group/btn flex space-x-2 items-center justify-start px-4 w-full text-black rounded-md h-10 font-medium shadow-input bg-gray-50 dark:bg-zinc-900 dark:shadow-[0px_0px_1px_1px_var(--neutral-800)]"
                   type="submit"
                 >
                   <IconBrandGoogle className="h-4 w-4 text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300" />
                   <span className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 text-sm">
                     Google
                   </span>
                   <BottomGradient />
                 </button>
                 <button
                   className=" relative group/btn flex space-x-2 items-center justify-start px-4 w-full text-black rounded-md h-10 font-medium shadow-input bg-gray-50 dark:bg-zinc-900 dark:shadow-[0px_0px_1px_1px_var(--neutral-800)]"
                   type="submit"
                 >
                   <IconBrandOnlyfans className="h-4 w-4 text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300" />
                   <span className="text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300 text-sm">
                     OnlyFans
                   </span>
                   <BottomGradient />
                 </button>
               </div>
             </form>
           </div>
         );
       }
       
       const BottomGradient = () => {
         return (
           <>
             <span className="group-hover/btn:opacity-100 block transition duration-500 opacity-0 absolute h-px w-full -bottom-px inset-x-0 bg-gradient-to-r from-transparent via-cyan-500 to-transparent" />
             <span className="group-hover/btn:opacity-100 blur-sm block transition duration-500 opacity-0 absolute h-px w-1/2 mx-auto -bottom-px inset-x-10 bg-gradient-to-r from-transparent via-indigo-500 to-transparent" />
           </>
         );
       };
       
       const LabelInputContainer = ({ children, className }: {
         children: React.ReactNode;
         className?: string;
       }) => {
         return (
           <div className={cn("flex flex-col space-y-2 w-full", className)}>
             {children}
           </div>
         );
       };

    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/spotlight
    2. Description: A spotlight effect with Tailwind CSS, good for drawing attention to a particular element on the page.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { Spotlight } from "./ui/spotlight";
       
       export function SpotlightPreview() {
         return (
           <div className="relative flex h-[40rem] w-full overflow-hidden rounded-md bg-black/[0.96]">
             <Spotlight
               className="-top-40 left-0 md:-top-20 md:left-60"
               fill="white"
             />
             <div className="relative z-10 mx-auto my-auto w-full max-w-7xl p-4 pt-20 md:pt-0">
               <h1 className="bg-opacity-50 bg-gradient-to-b from-neutral-50 to-neutral-400 bg-clip-text text-center text-4xl font-bold text-transparent md:text-7xl">
                 Spotlight <br /> is the new trend.
               </h1>
               <p className="mx-auto mt-4 max-w-lg text-center text-base font-normal text-neutral-300">
                 Spotlight effect is a great way to draw attention to a specific part of the page. Here, we are drawing the attention towards the text section of the page. I don't know why but I'm running out of copy.
               </p>
             </div>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "sticky-scroll-reveal.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/sticky-scroll-reveal.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/sticky-scroll-reveal"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "clsx", "tailwind-merge"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "content"
      type: "Array<{title: string"
      required: true
    - name: "description"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "content"
      type: "React.ReactNode}>"
      required: true
    - name: "contentClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This could be used to elegantly reveal the story behind a particular jewelry collection, or to showcase the craftsmanship and details of a specific piece as the user scrolls."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/sticky-scroll-reveal
    2. Description: A sticky container that sticks while scrolling, and text reveals on scroll. This component allows for a dynamic and engaging way to present content, where a section remains fixed while the user scrolls, with text or other elements appearing as they move down the page.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       '''
       "use client";
       import React from "react";
       import { StickyScroll } from "./ui/sticky-scroll-reveal";
       
       const content = [
           {
               title: "Title 1",
               description: "Description 1",
               content: <div>Content 1</div>
           },
           {
               title: "Title 2",
               description: "Description 2",
               content: <div>Content 2</div>
           }
       ];
       
       export function StickyScrollRevealDemo() {
         return (
           <div className="p-10">
             <StickyScroll content={content} />
           </div>
         );
       }
       '''

    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/svg-mask-effect
    2. Description: A mask reveal effect where hovering over a container reveals the content underneath. It can be used to create engaging and interactive sections on a webpage.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       
       "use client";
       import { MaskContainer } from "@/components/ui/svg-mask-effect";
       
       export function SVGMaskEffectDemo() {
         return (
           <div className="h-[40rem] w-full flex items-center justify-center  overflow-hidden">
             <MaskContainer
               revealText={
                 <p className="max-w-4xl mx-auto text-slate-800 text-center  text-4xl font-bold">
                   The first rule of MRR Club is you do not talk about MRR Club. The
                   second rule of MRR Club is you DO NOT talk about MRR Club.
                 </p>
               }
               className="h-[40rem] rounded-md border"
             >
               Discover the power of{" "}
               <span className="text-blue-500">Tailwind CSS v4</span> with native CSS
               variables and container queries with{" "}
               <span className="text-blue-500">advanced animations</span>.
             </MaskContainer>
           </div>
         );
       }

    - name: "activeTabClassName"

      required: false
    - name: "tabClassName"

      required: false
    - name: "contentClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: false
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: false
    - name: "active"
      type: "Tab"
      required: false
    - name: "hovering"
      type: "boolean"
      required: false
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used on a luxury jewelry website to elegantly switch between product categories like 'Rings', 'Necklaces', and 'Earrings', or to display different facets of a single product, such as 'Details', 'Specifications', and 'Craftsmanship'."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/tabs
    2. Description: Animated Tabs is a component that allows users to switch between different content sections with a smooth background animation.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       '''"use client";
       
       import { Tabs } from "../ui/tabs";
       
       export function TabsDemo() {
         const tabs = [
           {
             title: "Product",
             value: "product",
             content: (
               <div className="w-full overflow-hidden relative h-full rounded-2xl p-10 text-xl md:text-4xl font-bold text-white bg-gradient-to-br from-purple-700 to-violet-900">
                 <p>Product Tab</p>
                 <DummyContent />
               </div>
             ),
           },
           {
             title: "Services",
             value: "services",
             content: (
               <div className="w-full overflow-hidden relative h-full rounded-2xl p-10 text-xl md:text-4xl font-bold text-white bg-gradient-to-br from-purple-700 to-violet-900">
                 <p>Services tab</p>
                 <DummyContent />
               </div>
             ),
           },
         ];
       
         return (
           <div className="h-[20rem] md:h-[40rem] [perspective:1000px] relative b flex flex-col max-w-5xl mx-auto w-full  items-start justify-start my-40">
             <Tabs tabs={tabs} />
           </div>
         );
       }
       '''

- name: "tailwind-css-buttons.tsx"

  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: false
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: false
    - name: "onClick"
      type: "() => void"
      required: false
  customization_points:
    - "The diverse set of button styles can be used for various calls-to-action (CTAs) on a luxury jewelry website, such as 'Add to Bag', 'Book a Viewing', or 'Discover the Collection'. The more animated and unique button styles can be reserved for primary CTAs to create a more engaging user experience."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/tailwindcss-buttons-demo
    2. Description: A curated collection of versatile and battle-tested button components for Tailwind CSS, offering a wide variety of styles from simple and clean to animated and visually expressive.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import React from "react";
       import { IconClipboard } from "@tabler/icons-react";
       import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
       
       export const ButtonsCard = ({
         children,
         className,
         onClick,
       }: {
         children?: React.ReactNode;
         className?: string;
         onClick?: () => void;
       }) => {
         return (
           <div
             onClick={onClick}
             className={cn(
               "h-60 w-full bg-white rounded-xl border border-neutral-100 dark:bg-black dark:border-white/[0.2] hover:border-neutral-200 group/btn overflow-hidden relative flex items-center justify-center",
               className
             )}
           >
             <div className="absolute inset-0 dark:bg-dot-white/[0.1] bg-dot-black/[0.1]" />
             <IconClipboard className="absolute top-2 right-2 text-neutral-300 group-hover/btn:block hidden h-4 w-4 transition duration-200" />
             <div className="relative z-40">{children}</div>
           </div>
         );
       };

- name: "tracing-beam.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/tracing-beam.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/tracing-beam"
  npm_dependencies: ["framer-motion", "tailwind-merge", "clsx"]
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "children"
      type: "React.ReactNode"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component could be used on a luxury jewelry website to create a visually stunning narrative for the brand's history or the intricate process of crafting a piece of jewelry. It would guide the user's attention and add a touch of elegance and sophistication to the storytelling."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/tracing-beam
    2. Description: A beam that follows the path of an SVG as the user scrolls. The length of the beam adjusts with the scroll speed, creating a dynamic and engaging visual effect. It's ideal for storytelling or guiding users through content in a linear fashion.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       '''
       "use client";
       import React from "react";
       import { calsans } from "@/fonts/calsans";
       import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge";
       import { TracingBeam } from "@/components/ui/tracing-beam";
       
       export function TracingBeamDemo() {
         return (
           <TracingBeam className="px-6">
             <div className="max-w-2xl mx-auto antialiased pt-4 relative">
               {dummyContent.map((item, index) => (
                 <div key={`content-${index}`} className="mb-10">
                   <h2 className="bg-black text-white rounded-full text-sm w-fit px-4 py-1 mb-4">
                     {item.badge}
                   </h2>
                   <p className={twMerge(calsans.className, "text-xl mb-4")}>
                     {item.title}
                   </p>
                   <div className="text-sm  prose prose-sm dark:prose-invert">
                     {item?.image && (
                       <Image
                         src={item.image}
                         alt="blog thumbnail"
                         height="1000"
                         width="1000"
                         className="rounded-lg mb-10 object-cover"
                       />
                     )}
                     {item.description}
                   </div>
                 </div>
               ))}
             </div>
           </TracingBeam>
         );
       }
       '''

- name: "typewriter-effect.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/typewriter-effect.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/typewriter-effect"
  npm_dependencies: []
  used_in_concepts: ["01-vault", "10-maison"]
  props:
    - name: "className"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "words"
      type: "Array<{text: string; className: string;}>"
      required: true
    - name: "cursorClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "The typewriter effect can be used to create a sense of anticipation and draw attention to key messages on a luxury jewelry website. It could be used for headlines, taglines, or to reveal special offers in a dramatic and engaging way."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/typewriter-effect
    2. Description: Text generates as if it is being typed on the screen.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       "use client";
       import { TypewriterEffectSmooth } from "../ui/typewriter-effect";
       export function TypewriterEffectSmoothDemo() {
         const words = [
           {
             text: "Build",
           },
           {
             text: "awesome",
           },
           {
             text: "apps",
           },
           {
             text: "with",
           },
           {
             text: "Aceternity.",
             className: "text-blue-500 dark:text-blue-500",
           },
         ];
         return (
           <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center h-[40rem]  ">
             <p className="text-neutral-600 dark:text-neutral-200 text-xs sm:text-base  ">
               The road to freedom starts from here
             </p>
             <TypewriterEffectSmooth words={words} />
             <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row space-y-4 md:space-y-0 space-x-0 md:space-x-4">
               <button className="w-40 h-10 rounded-xl bg-black border dark:border-white border-transparent text-white text-sm">
                 Join now
               </button>
               <button className="w-40 h-10 rounded-xl bg-white text-black border border-black  text-sm">
                 Signup
               </button>
             </div>
           </div>
         );
       }

- name: "vortex-background.tsx"
  path: "src/components/ui/vortex-background.tsx"
  source: "aceternity"
  source_url: "https://ui.aceternity.com/components/vortex"
  npm_dependencies: ["clsx", "framer-motion", "tailwind-merge"]

    - name: "children"
      type: "any"

      required: true
    - name: "containerClassName"
      type: "string"
      required: true
    - name: "particleCount"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "rangeY"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "baseHue"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "baseSpeed"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "rangeSpeed"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "baseRadius"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "rangeRadius"
      type: "number"
      required: true
    - name: "backgroundColor"
      type: "string"
      required: true
  customization_points:
    - "This component can be used as a captivating background for the hero section of a luxury jewelry website, or to highlight a specific high-end collection, creating a sense of elegance and mystery."
    - "Colors and typography to match Vault Maison palette"
  ai_instructions: |
    To modify this component:
    1. Install via: npx shadcn@latest add @aceternity/vortex
    2. Description: A wavy, swirly, vortex background ideal for CTAs and backgrounds.
    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5
    4. Basic Usage:
       import { Vortex } from "@/components/ui/vortex";
       
       export function VortexDemo() {
         return (
           <div className="w-full mx-auto rounded-md  h-screen overflow-hidden">
             <Vortex
               backgroundColor="black"
               className="flex items-center flex-col justify-center px-2 md:px-10 py-4 w-full h-full"
             >
               <h2 className="text-white text-2xl md:text-6xl font-bold text-center">
                 The hell is this?
               </h2>
               <p className="text-white text-sm md:text-2xl max-w-xl mt-6 text-center">
                 This is chemical burn. It'll hurt more than you've ever been burned and
                 you'll have a scar.
               </p>
               <div className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-center gap-4 mt-6">
                 <button className="px-4 py-2 bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 transition duration-200 rounded-lg text-white shadow-[0px_2px_0px_0px_#FFFFFF40_inset]">
                   Order now
                 </button>
                 <button className="px-4 py-2  text-white ">Watch trailer</button>
               </div>
             </Vortex>
           </div>
         );
       }

    3. Luxury Relevance: 4/5

             <p className="text-2xl md:text-4xl lg:text-7xl text-white font-bold inter-var text-center">

             </p>
             <p className="text-base md:text-lg mt-4 text-white font-normal inter-var text-center">
